architecture.summary: Wire existing modules: health-check.ts into sync-setup, resolveExternalBranchPrefix into GitConfig branch naming, pr-linker into PR-based closure. New sync-health CLI command. No new services needed — pure integration of existing components.

integrations.summary: JIRA: addRemoteLink() in jira-client.ts fully implemented. ADO: addHyperlink() in ado-client.ts fully implemented. GitHub: gh CLI auth check exists. All 3 provider health checks exist in integration-health-check.ts. PR linking orchestrated by pr-linker.ts.

ui-ux.summary: CLI-only interface. formatHealthCheckResults() already renders pass/warn/fail with icons. sync-health adds --json for CI. Branch naming is transparent to user. No UI components involved.

performance.summary: Health checks make HTTP calls to JIRA/ADO APIs — already uses fetch with timeouts. sync-setup runs checks once at end. sync-health is on-demand. PR linking is per-PR (1-3 API calls). No batch or polling concerns.

security.summary: Credentials read from .env (JIRA_API_TOKEN, JIRA_EMAIL, ADO_PAT) — never logged or exposed. Health checks only verify auth, dont expose tokens. Branch names use ticket keys (public identifiers). No new secret handling needed.

edge-cases.summary: Missing credentials: health check returns fail with fix suggestion. No external ticket: branch naming falls back to static prefix. API failures in PR linking: logged as warnings, non-blocking. No providers configured: sync-health exits code 2. Branch name special chars: JIRA keys are alphanumeric, ADO uses AB# format.
